reasoning: As of mid-October 2025, Elon Musk was estimated to have a net worth of around $453 billion, holding a substantial ~$86 billion lead over the #2 billionaire, Larry Ellison (~$367 billion). While Ellison briefly overtook Musk in September 2025, Musk's net worth rebounded strongly. Tesla's Q3 2025 earnings reported on October 22 were disappointing, with net income falling 37%, which led to a 4.4% drop in TSLA stock on October 23. This shaves $10–15 billion off Musk's net worth but leaves him with a comfortable $70+ billion buffer over Ellison going into the final two months of the year. Given that the next TSLA earnings report won't occur until January 2026, and no major predictable catalysts exist to disrupt his large lead before December 31, 2025, the base rate of maintaining the #1 spot is extremely high. I have adjusted the October 16 prediction market estimate of 0.86 down slightly to 0.84 to account for the negative momentum following the weak Q3 earnings.
